blocks.content:
When you save the data source, Grafana issues a test query to Tempo using the URL and credentials you configured. A success message confirms that Grafana can reach your Tempo backend and that authentication is working.

To save and test the connection, complete the following step:

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Data source settings page Save and Test button']
blocks.content:
Click **Save & test**.

When successful, a green banner confirms the connection. If the test fails, Grafana shows an error describing the problem — for example, an unreachable URL, an invalid token, or a missing tenant header.
In the next milestone, you verify that trace data flows through to Explore.

---

### Troubleshooting

Explore the following troubleshooting topics if you need help:

- [Troubleshoot Tempo data source issues](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/tempo/troubleshooting/)
- [Streaming issues](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/tempo/troubleshooting/#streaming-issues)
- [Private data source connections](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/connect-externally-hosted/private-data-source-connect/)

---

### More to explore (optional)

- [Configure the Tempo data source](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/tempo/configure-tempo-data-source/)
- [Provision the Tempo data source](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/tempo/configure-tempo-data-source/provision/)
